name: Build Orchestrator
description: Recognize build, code-change, UI-change, debugging, validation, commit, push, and deployment-prep requests and route them to safe build planning or local execution.
purpose: Route build, code-change, UI-change, debugging, validation, commit, push, and deployment-prep requests to the correct workflow while preserving approval gates.
requiredContext: Whether the user wants plan-only or implementation
Available local worker / Hermes Nous runtime state if relevant
missingContextQuestions: What exact area should be changed?
Should this be plan-only or implemented now?
Should this be approved before commit, push, or deploy?
safetyRules: Prefer plan-first unless user explicitly asks to implement.
Never modify auth, middleware, migrations, or deployment config without explicit approval.
Run builds/tests first if workflow supports it before marking ready.

outputContract.format: Build intent detected -> Target system area -> Risk level -> Files to inspect -> Proposed plan -> Execution path -> Validation commands -> Approval needed before durable action.

positiveExamples: Fix the Skills page date bug.
Create a route for provider setup.
negativeExamples: Rewrite this email to my manager.
What should HR do about an I-9 TNC?
Tell me how routes work without changing anything.

evaluationPrompts.reason: Explicit feature request with build intent.

evaluationPrompts.input: Fix the Skills page date bug.

evaluationPrompts.reason: Direct commit/push request with approval gate.
Implementation request involving the local worker.
Ambiguous code-change request needs repo context.
Mixed-domain UI fix with strong build intent.
Validation should route to build-validation-runner as primary.
Deployment status should route to build-validation-runner as primary.

evaluationPrompts.input: Rewrite my email to my manager.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Email polish is not a build skill match.
HR drafting is not a build match.

evaluationPrompts.input: What should HR do after an I-9 TNC?
evaluationPrompts.reason: HR compliance question should not go to build.
Calendar/task automation is not a build route.
